mapping.a_element: number of binary nucleon-nucleon collisions (ν) as a measure of centrality
mapping.b_element: centrality classes in the clustering of color sources model

mapping.a_element: transverse momentum (p_T) spectra of charged hadrons
mapping.b_element: transverse momentum (p_T) dependence of azimuthal asymmetries (v2)

mapping.a_element: saturation of yield per nucleon-nucleon collision for high momentum particles
mapping.b_element: nuclear modification factor's dependence on azimuthal angle and centrality

mapping.a_element: spectator nucleon tagging for event selection
mapping.b_element: clustering of color sources as a framework for partonic interactions

brief_justification: While both papers study particle production in high-energy collisions and use centrality as a variable, paper A focuses on binary nucleon-nucleon collision scaling and yield saturation, whereas paper B models collective partonic interactions via color source clustering to explain azimuthal asymmetries (v2). The core mechanisms—binary collision scaling vs. collective flow from partonic clustering—are distinct.
